# solution.py
"""
OfficeTaskScheduler - Project Organizer Implementation.

Modules:
- Task Creation/Management (TaskService)
- Scheduling (deadline handling, upcoming deadline checks)
- Resource/Assignment (assign tasks, authorization)
- Real-Time Notifications (NotificationService)
- UI/Dashboard + Reports (get_dashboard, ReportService)
"""

import copy
import enum
import hashlib
import re
import secrets
import threading
import uuid
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import Optional


# ===========================================================================
# Exceptions
# ===========================================================================

class OfficeTaskSchedulerError(Exception):
    """Base exception for all OfficeTaskScheduler errors."""
    pass


class ValidationError(OfficeTaskSchedulerError):
    """Raised when input validation fails."""
    pass


class AuthenticationError(OfficeTaskSchedulerError):
    """Raised when authentication fails."""
    pass


class AuthorizationError(OfficeTaskSchedulerError):
    """Raised when authorization fails."""
    pass


class NotFoundError(OfficeTaskSchedulerError):
    """Raised when a requested resource is not found."""
    pass


class DuplicateError(OfficeTaskSchedulerError):
    """Raised when a duplicate resource is detected."""
    pass


# ===========================================================================
# Enums
# ===========================================================================

class Priority(enum.Enum):
    """Task priority levels."""
    LOW = 1
    MEDIUM = 2
    HIGH = 3
    URGENT = 4


class TaskStatus(enum.Enum):
    """Task status values."""
    PENDING = "pending"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"
    CANCELLED = "cancelled"


class NotificationType(enum.Enum):
    """Notification type categories."""
    TASK_ASSIGNED = "task_assigned"
    STATUS_CHANGED = "status_changed"
    DEADLINE_UPCOMING = "deadline_upcoming"
    TASK_COMPLETED = "task_completed"


class NotificationChannel(enum.Enum):
    """Notification delivery channels."""
    IN_APP = "in_app"
    EMAIL = "email"


# ===========================================================================
# Coercion helpers
# ===========================================================================

def _coerce_priority(value):
    """Coerce a value to a Priority enum member."""
    if isinstance(value, Priority):
        return value
    if isinstance(value, int):
        for p in Priority:
            if p.value == value:
                return p
        raise ValidationError("Invalid priority value: " + str(value))
    if isinstance(value, str):
        upper = value.upper()
        for p in Priority:
            if p.name == upper:
                return p
        raise ValidationError("Invalid priority: " + value)
    raise ValidationError("Invalid priority type")


def _coerce_status(value):
    """Coerce a value to a TaskStatus enum member."""
    if isinstance(value, TaskStatus):
        return value
    if isinstance(value, str):
        upper = value.upper()
        for s in TaskStatus:
            if s.name == upper:
                return s
        lower = value.lower()
        for s in TaskStatus:
            if s.value == lower:
                return s
        raise ValidationError("Invalid status: " + value)
    raise ValidationError("Invalid status type")


# ===========================================================================
# Data classes
# ===========================================================================

@dataclass
class User:
    """Represents a registered user."""
    id: str
    email: str
    display_name: str
    password_hash: str
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Task:
    """Represents a task."""
    id: str
    title: str
    creator_id: str
    assignee_id: Optional[str]
    priority: Priority
    status: TaskStatus
    deadline: Optional[datetime]
    created_at: datetime
    updated_at: datetime
    comment_ids: list = field(default_factory=list)


@dataclass
class Comment:
    """Represents a comment on a task."""
    id: str
    task_id: str
    author_id: str
    text: str
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Notification:
    """Represents a notification."""
    id: str
    recipient_id: str
    type: NotificationType
    channel: NotificationChannel
    message: str
    related_task_id: Optional[str]
    is_read: bool
    created_at: datetime


# ===========================================================================
# Email validation
# ===========================================================================

_EMAIL_RE = re.compile(r"^[^@\s]+@[^@\s]+\.[^@\s]+$")


# ===========================================================================
# AuthService
# ===========================================================================

class AuthService:
    """Handles user registration, login, session management."""

    def __init__(self, lock, users_by_id, users_by_email, sessions):
        self._lock = lock
        self._users_by_id = users_by_id
        self._users_by_email = users_by_email
        self._sessions = sessions

    def register(self, email, password, display_name, now=None):
        """Register a new user with email, password, and display name."""
        with self._lock:
            if now is None:
                now = datetime.now()
            if not _EMAIL_RE.match(email):
                raise ValidationError("Invalid email format")
            if not password:
                raise ValidationError("Password cannot be empty")
            if not display_name or not display_name.strip():
                raise ValidationError("Display name cannot be empty")
            email_lower = email.lower()
            if email_lower in self._users_by_email:
                raise DuplicateError("Email already registered")
            salt = secrets.token_hex(16)
            hash_val = hashlib.sha256((salt + password).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
            password_hash = salt + ":" + hash_val
            user_id = uuid.uuid4().hex
            user = User(
                id=user_id,
                email=email,
                display_name=display_name,
                password_hash=password_hash,
                created_at=now,
            )
            self._users_by_id[user_id] = user
            self._users_by_email[email_lower] = user_id
            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def login(self, email, password):
        """Authenticate a user and return a session token."""
        with self._lock:
            email_lower = email.lower()
            user_id = self._users_by_email.get(email_lower)
            if user_id is None:
                raise AuthenticationError("Unknown email")
            user = self._users_by_id[user_id]
            salt, expected_hash = user.password_hash.split(":", 1)
            actual_hash = hashlib.sha256((salt + password).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
            if actual_hash != expected_hash:
                raise AuthenticationError("Wrong password")
            token = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
            self._sessions[token] = user_id
            return token

    def validate_session(self, token):
        """Validate a session token and return the associated user."""
        with self._lock:
            user_id = self._sessions.get(token)
            if user_id is None:
                raise AuthenticationError("Invalid or expired token")
            user = self._users_by_id.get(user_id)
            if user is None:
                raise AuthenticationError("User not found for token")
            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def logout(self, token):
        """Invalidate a session token. No-op if token unknown."""
        with self._lock:
            self._sessions.pop(token, None)

    def get_user(self, user_id):
        """Get a user by their ID."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._users_by_id.get(user_id)
            if user is None:
                raise NotFoundError("User not found")
            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def user_exists(self, user_id):
        """Check if a user exists by ID."""
        with self._lock:
            return user_id in self._users_by_id


# ===========================================================================
# NotificationService
# ===========================================================================

class NotificationService:
    """Handles notification creation, retrieval, and management."""

    def __init__(self, lock, notifications_by_user, notifications_by_id, auth, tasks_ref):
        self._lock = lock
        self._notifications_by_user = notifications_by_user
        self._notifications_by_id = notifications_by_id
        self._auth = auth
        self._tasks_ref = tasks_ref

    def _emit(self, recipient_id, ntype, message, related_task_id, now):
        """Create IN_APP and EMAIL notifications for a recipient."""
        result = []
        for channel in (NotificationChannel.IN_APP, NotificationChannel.EMAIL):
            notif = Notification(
                id=uuid.uuid4().hex,
                recipient_id=recipient_id,
                type=ntype,
                channel=channel,
                message=message,
                related_task_id=related_task_id,
                is_read=False,
                created_at=now,
            )
            self._notifications_by_id[notif.id] = notif
            if recipient_id not in self._notifications_by_user:
                self._notifications_by_user[recipient_id] = []
            self._notifications_by_user[recipient_id].append(notif)
            result.append(notif)
        return result

    def get_notifications(self, token, unread_only=False):
        """Get notifications for the authenticated user."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            user_notifs = self._notifications_by_user.get(user.id, [])
            if unread_only:
                user_notifs = [n for n in user_notifs if not n.is_read]
            return [copy.deepcopy(n) for n in user_notifs]

    def mark_read(self, token, notif_id):
        """Mark a specific notification as read."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            notif = self._notifications_by_id.get(notif_id)
            if notif is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Notification not found")
            if notif.recipient_id != user.id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Not your notification")
            notif.is_read = True
            return copy.deepcopy(notif)

    def mark_all_read(self, token):
        """Mark all unread notifications for the user as read."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            user_notifs = self._notifications_by_user.get(user.id, [])
            count = 0
            for n in user_notifs:
                if not n.is_read:
                    n.is_read = True
                    count += 1
            return count

    def check_upcoming_deadlines(self, token, within_hours=24, now=None):
        """Check for tasks with upcoming deadlines and emit notifications."""
        with self._lock:
            if now is None:
                now = datetime.now()
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            window_end = now + timedelta(hours=within_hours)
            new_notifs = []
            tasks_store = self._tasks_ref.get("store")
            if tasks_store is None:
                return []
            for task in tasks_store.values():
                if task.assignee_id != user.id:
                    continue
                if task.deadline is None:
                    continue
                if task.status in (TaskStatus.COMPLETED, TaskStatus.CANCELLED):
                    continue
                if now <= task.deadline <= window_end:
                    emitted = self._emit(
                        user.id,
                        NotificationType.DEADLINE_UPCOMING,
                        "Deadline upcoming for task: " + task.title,
                        task.id,
                        now,
                    )
                    new_notifs.extend(emitted)
            return [copy.deepcopy(n) for n in new_notifs]


# ===========================================================================
# TaskService
# ===========================================================================

class TaskService:
    """Handles task creation, assignment, status updates, and comments."""

    def __init__(self, lock, tasks_by_id, comments_by_task, auth, notifications):
        self._lock = lock
        self._tasks_by_id = tasks_by_id
        self._comments_by_task = comments_by_task
        self._auth = auth
        self._notifications = notifications

    def _authorize(self, user, task):
        """Check if a user is authorized to access a task."""
        if user.id != task.creator_id and user.id != task.assignee_id:
            raise AuthorizationError("Not authorized for this task")

    def create_task(self, token, title, priority=Priority.MEDIUM, deadline=None, assignee_id=None, now=None):
        """Create a new task with the given parameters."""
        with self._lock:
            if now is None:
                now = datetime.now()
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            if not title or not title.strip():
                raise ValidationError("Title cannot be empty")
            if deadline is not None and deadline < now:
                raise ValidationError("Deadline cannot be in the past")
            if assignee_id is not None and not self._auth.user_exists(assignee_id):
                raise NotFoundError("Assignee user not found")
            priority = _coerce_priority(priority)
            task = Task(
                id=uuid.uuid4().hex,
                title=title,
                creator_id=user.id,
                assignee_id=assignee_id,
                priority=priority,
                status=TaskStatus.PENDING,
                deadline=deadline,
                created_at=now,
                updated_at=now,
                comment_ids=[],
            )
            self._tasks_by_id[task.id] = task
            if assignee_id is not None and assignee_id != user.id:
                self._notifications._emit(
                    assignee_id,
                    NotificationType.TASK_ASSIGNED,
                    "You have been assigned task: " + title,
                    task.id,
                    now,
                )
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def assign_task(self, token, task_id, assignee_id, now=None):
        """Assign a task to a user."""
        with self._lock:
            if now is None:
                now = datetime.now()
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            task = self._tasks_by_id.get(task_id)
            if task is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Task not found")
            self._authorize(user, task)
            if not self._auth.user_exists(assignee_id):
                raise NotFoundError("Assignee user not found")
            task.assignee_id = assignee_id
            task.updated_at = now
            if assignee_id != user.id:
                self._notifications._emit(
                    assignee_id,
                    NotificationType.TASK_ASSIGNED,
                    "You have been assigned task: " + task.title,
                    task.id,
                    now,
                )
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def set_priority(self, token, task_id, priority, now=None):
        """Set the priority of a task."""
        with self._lock:
            if now is None:
                now = datetime.now()
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            task = self._tasks_by_id.get(task_id)
            if task is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Task not found")
            self._authorize(user, task)
            priority = _coerce_priority(priority)
            task.priority = priority
            task.updated_at = now
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def set_deadline(self, token, task_id, deadline, now=None):
        """Set the deadline of a task."""
        with self._lock:
            if now is None:
                now = datetime.now()
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            task = self._tasks_by_id.get(task_id)
            if task is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Task not found")
            self._authorize(user, task)
            if deadline < now:
                raise ValidationError("Deadline cannot be in the past")
            task.deadline = deadline
            task.updated_at = now
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def update_status(self, token, task_id, status, now=None):
        """Update the status of a task and notify the other party."""
        with self._lock:
            if now is None:
                now = datetime.now()
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            task = self._tasks_by_id.get(task_id)
            if task is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Task not found")
            self._authorize(user, task)
            status = _coerce_status(status)
            task.status = status
            task.updated_at = now
            if user.id == task.assignee_id:
                notify_id = task.creator_id
            else:
                notify_id = task.assignee_id
            if notify_id is not None and notify_id != user.id:
                self._notifications._emit(
                    notify_id,
                    NotificationType.STATUS_CHANGED,
                    "Task status changed to " + status.value + ": " + task.title,
                    task.id,
                    now,
                )
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def get_task(self, token, task_id):
        """Get a task by ID with authorization check."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            task = self._tasks_by_id.get(task_id)
            if task is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Task not found")
            self._authorize(user, task)
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def get_dashboard(self, token, now=None):
        """Get dashboard rows for tasks assigned to the authenticated user."""
        with self._lock:
            if now is None:
                now = datetime.now()
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            rows = []
            for task in self._tasks_by_id.values():
                if task.assignee_id != user.id:
                    continue
                is_overdue = (
                    task.deadline is not None
                    and task.status not in (TaskStatus.COMPLETED, TaskStatus.CANCELLED)
                    and task.deadline < now
                )
                rows.append({
                    "task_id": task.id,
                    "title": task.title,
                    "status": task.status,
                    "priority": task.priority,
                    "deadline": task.deadline,
                    "is_overdue": is_overdue,
                })
            return rows

    def add_comment(self, token, task_id, text, now=None):
        """Add a comment to a task."""
        with self._lock:
            if now is None:
                now = datetime.now()
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            task = self._tasks_by_id.get(task_id)
            if task is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Task not found")
            self._authorize(user, task)
            if not text or not text.strip():
                raise ValidationError("Comment text cannot be empty")
            comment = Comment(
                id=uuid.uuid4().hex,
                task_id=task_id,
                author_id=user.id,
                text=text,
                created_at=now,
            )
            if task_id not in self._comments_by_task:
                self._comments_by_task[task_id] = []
            self._comments_by_task[task_id].append(comment)
            task.comment_ids.append(comment.id)
            task.updated_at = now
            return copy.deepcopy(comment)

    def get_comments(self, token, task_id):
        """Get all comments for a task in insertion order."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            task = self._tasks_by_id.get(task_id)
            if task is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Task not found")
            self._authorize(user, task)
            comments = self._comments_by_task.get(task_id, [])
            return [copy.deepcopy(c) for c in comments]

    def list_assigned_tasks(self, token, user_id=None):
        """List tasks assigned to a user."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            if user_id is not None and user_id != user.id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Cannot view other user tasks")
            target_id = user_id if user_id is not None else user.id
            result = []
            for task in self._tasks_by_id.values():
                if task.assignee_id == target_id:
                    result.append(copy.deepcopy(task))
            return result

    def list_created_tasks(self, token, user_id=None):
        """List tasks created by a user."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            if user_id is not None and user_id != user.id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Cannot view other user tasks")
            target_id = user_id if user_id is not None else user.id
            result = []
            for task in self._tasks_by_id.values():
                if task.creator_id == target_id:
                    result.append(copy.deepcopy(task))
            return result

    def get_tasks_for_user(self, token, user_id=None):
        """Get all tasks related to a user (created or assigned)."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            if user_id is not None and user_id != user.id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Cannot view other user tasks")
            target_id = user_id if user_id is not None else user.id
            seen = set()
            result = []
            for task in self._tasks_by_id.values():
                if task.creator_id == target_id or task.assignee_id == target_id:
                    if task.id not in seen:
                        seen.add(task.id)
                        result.append(copy.deepcopy(task))
            return result


# ===========================================================================
# ReportService
# ===========================================================================

class ReportService:
    """Generates reports and analytics on tasks."""

    def __init__(self, lock, tasks_by_id, auth):
        self._lock = lock
        self._tasks_by_id = tasks_by_id
        self._auth = auth

    def _completion_rate(self, user_id):
        """Calculate completion rate for a user (caller must hold lock)."""
        total = 0
        completed = 0
        for task in self._tasks_by_id.values():
            if task.creator_id == user_id:
                total += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    completed += 1
        rate = round(completed / total, 4) if total > 0 else 0.0
        return {"total": total, "completed": completed, "rate": rate}

    def _overdue_tasks(self, user_id, now):
        """Get overdue tasks for a user (caller must hold lock)."""
        rows = []
        for task in self._tasks_by_id.values():
            if task.creator_id != user_id:
                continue
            if task.deadline is None:
                continue
            if task.status in (TaskStatus.COMPLETED, TaskStatus.CANCELLED):
                continue
            if task.deadline < now:
                rows.append({
                    "task_id": task.id,
                    "title": task.title,
                    "assignee_id": task.assignee_id,
                    "deadline": task.deadline,
                    "priority": task.priority,
                    "status": task.status,
                })
        rows.sort(key=lambda r: r["deadline"])
        return rows

    def _task_distribution(self, user_id):
        """Get task distribution for a user (caller must hold lock)."""
        dist = {"unassigned": 0}
        for task in self._tasks_by_id.values():
            if task.creator_id != user_id:
                continue
            if task.assignee_id is None:
                dist["unassigned"] += 1
            else:
                if task.assignee_id not in dist:
                    dist[task.assignee_id] = 0
                dist[task.assignee_id] += 1
        return dist

    def completion_rate(self, token):
        """Calculate the completion rate of tasks created by the user."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            return self._completion_rate(user.id)

    def overdue_tasks(self, token, now=None):
        """Get overdue tasks created by the user, sorted by deadline ascending."""
        with self._lock:
            if now is None:
                now = datetime.now()
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            return self._overdue_tasks(user.id, now)

    def task_distribution(self, token):
        """Get distribution of tasks by assignee for tasks created by the user."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            return self._task_distribution(user.id)

    def summary(self, token, now=None):
        """Get a full summary report including completion, overdue, and distribution."""
        with self._lock:
            if now is None:
                now = datetime.now()
            user = self._auth.validate_session(token)
            return {
                "completion": self._completion_rate(user.id),
                "overdue": self._overdue_tasks(user.id, now),
                "distribution": self._task_distribution(user.id),
            }


# ===========================================================================
# Facade
# ===========================================================================

class OfficeTaskScheduler:
    """Main application facade coordinating all services."""

    def __init__(self):
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        users_by_id = {}
        users_by_email = {}
        sessions = {}
        tasks_by_id = {}
        comments_by_task = {}
        notifications_by_user = {}
        notifications_by_id = {}
        tasks_ref = {"store": tasks_by_id}

        self.auth = AuthService(self._lock, users_by_id, users_by_email, sessions)
        self.notifications = NotificationService(
            self._lock, notifications_by_user, notifications_by_id, self.auth, tasks_ref
        )
        self.tasks = TaskService(
            self._lock, tasks_by_id, comments_by_task, self.auth, self.notifications
        )
        self.reports = ReportService(self._lock, tasks_by_id, self.auth)


OfficeTaskSchedulerApp = OfficeTaskScheduler
